sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Roma are in excellent form, winning four of their last five matches, including a 2-1 victory over FC Midtjylland and a 3-1 win against Cremonese. Napoli have had mixed results, securing a 3-1 win over Atalanta but suffering a 2-0 loss to Bologna, indicating inconsistency in their recent performances.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between Roma and Napoli, matches have been tight, with a 1-1 draw in their most recent meeting on February 2, 2025, and a 1-0 Napoli win on November 24, 2024. Napoli have not lost to Roma in their last three head-to-head games, which could give them a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Roma, sitting 3rd with 27 points, boast the league's best defense, conceding only 6 goals, and dominate in possession (58.6%) and final third entries. Napoli, in 4th with 25 points, have scored more (19 goals) but their defense has been less solid, conceding 11 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Roma's Matías Soulé has been a standout with 4 goals and 2 assists, while Napoli will miss key midfielder Frank Anguissa, which could impact their midfield stability. Scott McTominay's recent form for Napoli, with 2 goals and 2 assists, will be crucial for their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Roma's strong home form and defensive solidity, coupled with Napoli's inconsistency and missing key players, Roma are slightly favored to win. However, Napoli's ability to score and their head-to-head edge suggest a close contest, aligning with Sofascore's 41% home win probability.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Napoli lead Roma by a single goal at the Stadio Olimpico. The match has been competitive, with Napoli striking through David Neres in the 36th minute. Roma have shown resilience but have failed to convert their chances, while Napoli have been more clinical in attack. Both teams have had their moments, but Napoli hold the upper hand going into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Napoli have dominated possession and shown greater intent in the final third, creating more dangerous opportunities. Their duel-winning ability has been impressive, allowing them to control key areas of the pitch. Roma, on the other hand, have struggled to assert themselves consistently, though they’ve had their fair share of entries into attacking zones. Defensively, Roma need to tighten up, as Napoli have exploited gaps effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Roma pushing forward in the early minutes, showing intent to unsettle Napoli. However, momentum quickly shifted to Napoli, who took control with sustained attacking spells around the midway point of the half. Roma fought back with a strong period of pressure just before the half-hour mark, looking dangerous at times. Napoli regained the upper hand leading up to their goal and maintained threatening attacks until the break, while Roma’s momentum waned toward the end.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Napoli, David Neres has been the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat with his movement and creativity. Rasmus Højlund has also been influential with his playmaking in the final third. On Roma’s side, Evan Ferguson has shown potential to be a game-changer despite missing a big chance, while Lorenzo Pellegrini’s vision could unlock Napoli’s defense if given space. Roma will need their key men to step up in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Roma are likely to come out with renewed energy, pushing for an equalizer early in the second half. They may focus on increasing their intensity in the final third and exploiting set-pieces. Napoli, meanwhile, might adopt a more cautious approach to protect their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities. If Roma can improve their finishing, they could turn the game around, but Napoli’s confidence and structure make them a tough opponent to break down.
